bins.name: Reject ELCC as Interconnection Credit
bins.desc: The filer opposes proposals to use regional ELCC values from resource adequacy markets as interconnection offset credit for VPPs. He argues the two frameworks are analytically incompatible: resource adequacy is a regional statistical mechanism while interconnection requires worst-case physical analysis at specific nodes. His Monte Carlo model shows a 15:1 median overestimation when regional ELCC is applied to local interconnection.
quotes: This comment demonstrates that such proposals fundamentally conflate two distinct grid reliability mechanisms that operate under entirely different engineering and regulatory frameworks: Resource Adequacy and Nodal Interconnection.

The application of Resource Adequacy metrics to interconnection engineering creates what I term a "Regulatory Hallucination"—a policy framework that appears mathematically valid on paper but lacks physical correspondence to actual grid conditions at the point of interconnection.

Interconnection studies serve an entirely different function: determining whether specific transmission and distribution equipment can safely accommodate a new point-to-point power flow without exceeding thermal, voltage, or stability limits under worst-case conditions.
The consequences of interconnection study failure are not financial—they are physical equipment damage, cascading outages, and potential loss of load.

Residential VPP resources are necessarily dispersed across residential neighborhoods throughout a utility service territory or RTO region. In PJM, a Demand Response aggregator might enroll 50,000 smart thermostats distributed across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Maryland, and Delaware. These devices provide regional load reduction, but they are not concentrated at the specific transmission node serving the data center.

Interconnection studies must model the worst-case hour for the specific local transmission node, which may not coincide with the regional system peak or the hours when VPP resources achieve maximum performance.

The median Attribution Gap of 4.72% indicates that a VPP receiving 69% ELCC credit in regional Resource Adequacy markets provides approximately 4.7% of that credit value for local interconnection purposes—a factor of 15:1 overestimation if regional ELCC is applied directly.

granting interconnection credit based on regional ELCC would result in overestimation by a factor of 7:1 or greater in 93% of cases, and overestimation by a factor of 3.5:1 or greater in virtually 100% of cases.

Attribution Gap is structural, not transient: The distribution parameters used in this analysis reflect mature VPP programs operating under best practices. The 4-6% median attribution is not a temporary limitation that will disappear with program refinement—it is an inherent consequence of applying regional statistical resources to local deterministic requirements.
The Commission should reject proposals to grant automatic interconnection offset credit based on Resource Adequacy ELCC values.

such contributions must be demonstrated through the same rigorous interconnection study methodology applied to all other resources: Locational specificity... Temporal certainty... Operational firmness... Verification and measurement

bins.name: Geographic Proximity Required
bins.desc: The filer argues that only VPP resources electrically proximate to the constrained node can provide genuine interconnection relief, consistent with AC power flow physics. Dispersed residential portfolios across a multi-state RTO footprint cannot relieve a specific substation constraint.
quotes: Residential VPP resources are necessarily dispersed across residential neighborhoods throughout a utility service territory or RTO region. In PJM, a Demand Response aggregator might enroll 50,000 smart thermostats distributed across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Maryland, and Delaware. These devices provide regional load reduction, but they are not concentrated at the specific transmission node serving the data center.
Load reduction at Location A only relieves constraints at Location B if A and B are electrically proximate in the network topology.

such contributions must be demonstrated through the same rigorous interconnection study methodology applied to all other resources: Locational specificity... Temporal certainty... Operational firmness... Verification and measurement

bins.name: Data Centers Fund Their Upgrades
bins.desc: The filer concludes that data centers should continue to fund the interconnection upgrades their load requires rather than receiving offset credit based on regional VPP statistics.
quotes: VPPs should continue to receive appropriate compensation through capacity markets for their genuine Resource Adequacy contributions. Data centers should continue to fund interconnection upgrades necessary to maintain physical system reliability.

bins.name: Keep VPPs in Capacity Markets
bins.desc: The filer supports VPPs receiving capacity market compensation for their genuine resource adequacy value, but opposes extending that role into interconnection engineering where different physical standards apply.
quotes: VPPs should continue to receive appropriate compensation through capacity markets for their genuine Resource Adequacy contributions. Data centers should continue to fund interconnection upgrades necessary to maintain physical system reliability.

bins.name: FERC Should Reject Automatic Offsets
bins.desc: The filer urges FERC to explicitly reject automatic ELCC-based interconnection offset credit, framing this as a matter of Commission jurisdiction to enforce its own interconnection engineering precedent from Orders 2003 and 2023.
quotes: The Commission should reject proposals to grant automatic interconnection offset credit based on Resource Adequacy ELCC values.

bins.name: Firm Commitments for Any DER Credit
bins.desc: If DER resources are to receive any interconnection credit, the filer argues they must meet the same contractual firmness, metering, and verification standards as generation interconnections, including binding financial penalties for non-performance.
quotes: such contributions must be demonstrated through the same rigorous interconnection study methodology applied to all other resources: Locational specificity... Temporal certainty... Operational firmness... Verification and measurement

bins.name: ELCC and Interconnection Are Incompatible
bins.desc: The central theme of the filing is that resource adequacy ELCC and interconnection engineering are fundamentally different analytical frameworks. Treating regional statistical performance as physical node-level capacity creates what the filer calls a Regulatory Hallucination that risks actual equipment damage.
quotes: This comment demonstrates that such proposals fundamentally conflate two distinct grid reliability mechanisms that operate under entirely different engineering and regulatory frameworks: Resource Adequacy and Nodal Interconnection.

The application of Resource Adequacy metrics to interconnection engineering creates what I term a "Regulatory Hallucination"—a policy framework that appears mathematically valid on paper but lacks physical correspondence to actual grid conditions at the point of interconnection.
The Commission should reject proposals to grant automatic interconnection offset credit based on Resource Adequacy ELCC values.

VPPs should continue to receive appropriate compensation through capacity markets for their genuine Resource Adequacy contributions. Data centers should continue to fund interconnection upgrades necessary to maintain physical system reliability.

bins.name: Monte Carlo Shows 15x Overestimation
bins.desc: The filer presents a stochastic model applying three sequential filters (locational, temporal, operational) to quantify the gap between regional ELCC and local interconnection value. The median result is 4.72% attribution, representing a factor of 15 to 1 overestimation. He finds the gap is structural and will not improve with program maturity.
quotes: The median Attribution Gap of 4.72% indicates that a VPP receiving 69% ELCC credit in regional Resource Adequacy markets provides approximately 4.7% of that credit value for local interconnection purposes—a factor of 15:1 overestimation if regional ELCC is applied directly.

granting interconnection credit based on regional ELCC would result in overestimation by a factor of 7:1 or greater in 93% of cases, and overestimation by a factor of 3.5:1 or greater in virtually 100% of cases.

Attribution Gap is structural, not transient: The distribution parameters used in this analysis reflect mature VPP programs operating under best practices. The 4-6% median attribution is not a temporary limitation that will disappear with program refinement—it is an inherent consequence of applying regional statistical resources to local deterministic requirements.

bins.name: Dispersed VPPs Cannot Relieve Local Nodes
bins.desc: VPP portfolios dispersed across a broad RTO footprint provide regional load reduction but cannot physically relieve constraints at a specific substation node. The locational filter in the filer's model has the strongest correlation with overall attribution degradation.
quotes: Residential VPP resources are necessarily dispersed across residential neighborhoods throughout a utility service territory or RTO region. In PJM, a Demand Response aggregator might enroll 50,000 smart thermostats distributed across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Maryland, and Delaware. These devices provide regional load reduction, but they are not concentrated at the specific transmission node serving the data center.
Load reduction at Location A only relieves constraints at Location B if A and B are electrically proximate in the network topology.

bins.name: Local and Regional Peaks Diverge
bins.desc: The local worst-case hour that drives an interconnection constraint may not coincide with regional system peaks that drive ELCC calculations. Seasonal variation, microclimate effects, and industrial load patterns can shift local peaks to hours when VPP resources are not performing.
quotes: Interconnection studies must model the worst-case hour for the specific local transmission node, which may not coincide with the regional system peak or the hours when VPP resources achieve maximum performance.

bins.name: Worst-Case Physical Standards Must Hold
bins.desc: The filer supports FERC's established interconnection study standards requiring worst-case physical analysis. He argues these standards reflect fundamental engineering requirements for thermal limits, conductor ampacity, and protection coordination that cannot be replaced by statistical or financial mechanisms.
quotes: Interconnection studies serve an entirely different function: determining whether specific transmission and distribution equipment can safely accommodate a new point-to-point power flow without exceeding thermal, voltage, or stability limits under worst-case conditions.

such contributions must be demonstrated through the same rigorous interconnection study methodology applied to all other resources: Locational specificity... Temporal certainty... Operational firmness... Verification and measurement

bins.name: Interconnection Failures Are Physical
bins.desc: Unlike resource adequacy shortfalls that are resolved through financial penalties and multi-year procurement, interconnection failures manifest immediately as equipment damage, voltage collapse, and cascading outages. The filer uses this distinction to argue why probabilistic VPP credit is inappropriate for interconnection.
quotes: The consequences of interconnection study failure are not financial—they are physical equipment damage, cascading outages, and potential loss of load.
